stimulus: Sand in the Sahara Desert is lifted skyward by seasonal updrafts, but the rotation of Earth and gusts from passing storm systems together push ______blank across the Atlantic to fertilize the soils of the Amazon rainforest—a phenomenon scientists call the Saharan dust plume.
stem: Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?
rationale:
Choice A is the best answer. The convention being tested is pronoun-antecedent agreement. The singular pronoun "it" agrees in number with the singular antecedent "sand" and clearly indicates that the sand—not the updrafts, gusts, or storm systems—is pushed across the Atlantic.

Choice B is incorrect. The plural pronoun "these" doesn’t agree in number with the singular antecedent "sand" and is ambiguous as to which plural noun in the sentence it refers. Choice C is incorrect. The plural pronoun "them" doesn’t agree in number with the singular antecedent "sand." Choice D is incorrect. The plural pronoun "those" doesn’t agree in number with the singular antecedent "sand."
